title: Stock Market Today (June 26, 2026): Nasdaq and S&P 500 tread water amid tech sell-off, reported OpenAI IPO delay
summary: Stock Market Today (June 26, 2026): Nasdaq and S&P 500 tread water amid tech sell-off, reported OpenAI IPO delay
extracted_text: S&P 500: +0.14% mid-day. Nasdaq and S&P 500 'tread water.' OpenAI IPO delay to 2027 at $1T valuation target. South Korea Kospi triggered circuit breaker after -8% intraday (-5.8% close). ON Semiconductor -22.58% on Synaptics $7B all-stock deal. Moderna +11.88% (S&P 500 top performer) on investor day CAR-T for autoimmune diseases. Acadia Pharma +12.16% on EU Daybue approval. Apple fell Thursday on price hikes. WTI $69.20 (-3.8%), Brent $72.47 (-3.7%), Gold $4,063.70 (+0.40%), Bitcoin $60,015.59 (+1.1%). DXY 101.20.

title: Schwab Market Update: Tech Malaise Persists Amid AI Cost, Spending Fears
summary: Schwab Market Update: Tech Malaise Persists Amid AI Cost, Spending Fears
extracted_text: S&P 500 7,357.49 (-0.01%), Dow 51,920.62 (+0.14%), Nasdaq 25,358.60 (-0.46%). 10Y yield 4.39%. DXY 101.20 (-0.23%). VIX 20.12 (+6.56%). WTI $69.55 (-3.30%). Kevin Gordon (SCFR): rolling 52-week correlation between cap-weighted and equal-weighted S&P 500 at lowest since 2003. Collin Martin (SCFR): record IG bond issuance this month — risk of spread widening if demand lags. Apple -6% Thursday worst day in over a year.

title: Treasury Yields Snapshot: June 26, 2026
extracted_text: 10-year note finished June 26, 2026 at 4.38%. 2-year note ended at 4.07%. 10Y/2Y spread: not inverted (10Y 4.38% > 2Y 4.07%). Most recent inversion: July 5, 2022 to August 26, 2024. Last negative reading: September 5, 2024. 30-year fixed mortgage: 6.49% (Freddie Mac).

title: Most brokerages see no Fed policy change this year
summary: Most brokerages see no Fed policy change this year
extracted_text: Majority (13 of 17) brokerages expect no policy change. BofA: 75 bps hikes (Sep, Oct, Dec) to 4.25-4.50%. Deutsche Bank: 50 bps hikes to 4.00-4.25%. Citi/Wells: 50 bps cuts. Fed funds at 3.50-3.75% since Dec 2025. Money markets pricing ~37 bps of hikes. Fed dot plot: 9 of 19 policymakers saw hike later this year. Kevin Warsh new Chair.

title: The Weekly Market Update – 6/22/26: New Fed Tilts Hawkish, Equities Mixed
summary: The Weekly Market Update – 6/22/26: New Fed Tilts Hawkish, Equities Mixed
extracted_text: June 17 FOMC: rates unchanged at 3.50-3.75%. Warsh shortened statement to 7 sentences. Final sentence: 'The Committee will deliver price stability.' SEP revealed hawkish dot plot. Two task forces announced: regulatory framework and monetary policy framework review.

title: Crude Oil Prices Today: Why Brent Is Holding Near $74
summary: Crude Oil Prices Today: Why Brent Is Holding Near $74
extracted_text: Brent $74.73 at 13:00 GMT+8 June 26. Below both MA50 (95.53 declining) and MA200 (78.75 declining). RSI 14 at 36.41. OPEC+ May +206K b/d, June +188K b/d, July +188K b/d planned. EIA slashed 2026 global oil demand growth by ~1.1M b/d. US Treasury 60-day general license for Iranian crude (valid until Aug 21, 2026). Cargo vessel struck off Oman June 25. Saudi Aramco CEO: market normalization may take until 2027.

title: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
summary: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
extracted_text: ON Semiconductor acquiring Synaptics in ~$7B all-stock deal. 1.350 ON shares per Synaptics share. TAM expands $30B to $243B by 2030. Edge AI / physical AI push. Deal approved unanimously by both boards. Close expected mid-2027 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

title: Consumer Sentiment Improves in June as Gasoline Prices Moderate
summary: Consumer Sentiment Improves in June as Gasoline Prices Moderate
extracted_text: Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index final June: 49.5, up from May 44.8 (+10.5% MoM). Current conditions: 47.7 (May 45.8). Still second lowest on record. Gasoline price moderation helped.

title: After-Hours Stock Movers — June 26, 2026
extracted_text: After-hours gainers: UPC +92.57%, SAGT +19.05%, ALPS +18.81%. Losers: TVRD -22.83%, SDOT -18.54%, TVGN -16.25%. Index after-hours: S&P 500 +0.44%, Nasdaq 100 +0.07%, Dow -0.05%, Russell 2000 +0.30%.

title: US Dollar Index Historical Data — June 26, 2026
summary: US Dollar Index Historical Data — June 26, 2026
extracted_text: DXY June 26: 101.35 (-0.08%), open 101.51, high 101.57, low 101.05. June 25: 101.43 (-0.18%). June 24: 101.61. 52-week range: 95.55 (Jan 27) to 101.80 (Jun 24). Dollar sliding amid crude weakness.
